chart.title: Palma ratio
chart.subtitle: The Palma ratio is a measure of inequality that divides the share received by the richest 10% by the share of the poorest 40%. Higher values indicate higher inequality. Inequality is measured here in terms of income before taxes and benefits.
chart.note: Income is measured before payment of taxes and non-pension benefits, but after the payment of public and private pensions.

country.citationShort: – processed by Our World in Data
